input.question: Is the word مَدْرَسَة masculine or feminine?
input.explanation: مَدْرَسَة ends with ة so it's feminine

input.question: Is the word بَيْت (bayt - house) masculine or feminine?
input.explanation: بَيْت has no ة ending, so it's masculine

input.question: What is the present tense form of 'I write' in Arabic?
Make this definite: كِتَاب
Complete with correct gender: مَدْرَسَة ___
input.explanation: Excellent! مَدْرَسَة is feminine so you used كَبِيرَة

input.question: Is the word مَدْرَسَة masculine or feminine?
input.explanation: مَدْرَسَة ends with ة (taa marbuuta), which makes it feminine

input.question: Is the word كِتَاب masculine or feminine?
input.explanation: كِتَاب has no ة ending, so it's masculine

input.question: Make this negative (present): أَكْتُب
input.explanation: Used ما (for past) instead of لا (for present)

input.question: Complete with correct gender: كِتَاب ___
input.explanation: كِتَاب is masculine, so we need كَبِير (not كَبِيرَة)

input.question: Make both definite: كِتَاب كَبِير
input.explanation: Both noun and adjective need ال for definiteness agreement
